hero.subtitle: Administrative CRUD over REST and GraphQL, dual-ready persistence, and authorization with the Role enum.

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: The users module owns the administrative user lifecycle. Public registration, email verification, and password reset live in the Auth module.

persistence.title: Persistence
items: TypeORM entity (PostgreSQL) — active store used by UsersService
Mongoose schema (MongoDB) — dual-ready; repository registered but not injected into the service
Cache invalidation with the users:* pattern on writes

surfaces.title: Surfaces
items: REST under the API prefix /users — JWT + roles required by the route
GraphQL — same operations, JWT + roles required by the operation

roles.title: Role enum
roles.description: The roles field is optional. When provided, each value must be a member of the Role enum. Customize the enum in src/modules/auth/enums/role.enum.ts for your project.

values.title: Role enum values
values.intro: These values are defined in src/modules/auth/enums/role.enum.ts. For new roles, extend the enum — do not use free-form strings.

authorization.title: Authorization
authorization.description: REST and GraphQL require a valid JWT. Protect operations with @Roles(...) using members of your Role enum. RolesGuard and JwtAuthGuard resolve the request for both HTTP and GraphQL.
authorization.graphql: GraphQL: guards on the resolver + @Roles(Role.YOUR_ROLE) on queries and mutations

dataModel.title: Data model
dataModel.description: Main user fields (IUser interface / entity / schema):
fields: password — bcrypt hash (not exposed in GraphQL)
isActive — default true (Auth registration creates inactive users)
roles — Role[] (Postgres default []; Mongo default follows your project's enum)

create.title: CreateUserDto
create.description: Required: firstName, lastName, username, email, password (@StrongPassword). Optional: isActive, roles (Role[]).

update.title: UpdateUserDto
update.description: All fields optional. roles validates with @IsEnum(Role, { each: true }). Email verification token fields exist on the DTO but are not GraphQL fields.

password.title: Strong password
password.description: @StrongPassword requires upper, lower, digit, special character, and blocks obvious sequences (e.g. abc, 123).

rest.title: REST API
rest.intro: API global prefix: use a Bearer JWT from a user that has the roles required by the routes.

create.title: Create user
create.description: Creates a user. Conflict (409) if email or username already exists.

update.title: Update
update.description: Partial update. Re-hashes password if sent and not already bcrypt.

delete.title: Delete
delete.description: Removes the user and returns a snapshot.

graphql.title: GraphQL
graphql.description: Operations on the app GraphQL path. Send Authorization: Bearer <token>. The schema exposes the Role enum. Returned fields: id, username, firstName, lastName, email, isActive, roles (password is not exposed).

businessRules.title: Business rules
items: Bcrypt with BCRYPT_HASH_FACTOR; skip re-hash if value is already bcrypt
Helpers: updateRoles, addRole, removeRole, replaceRole (typed with Role)
Cache @EnableCache / @CacheTTL(1800) on create, findAll, update, remove; @NoCache on point finders

auth.title: Auth
items: Public registration, email verification, login, forgot/reset password

passwordOmit.title: Password in responses
passwordOmit.description: REST GET and GraphQL do not expose password. The hash is loaded internally by Auth only when needed (login and password change).

extendEnum.title: Customize roles
extendEnum.description: Adjust the Role enum and @Roles(...) decorators on routes/resolvers. Do not send strings outside the enum.

samples.createBody: roles optional — Role enum values only
